title: Storms, lightning kill 16 in MP and J’khand: Debris buries homes, schools and vehicles in U’khand; mercury ... - Bhaskar English
full_text:
Cloudburst in Uttarakhand's Chamoli, debris buries homes, shops:Storms, lightning kill 16 in MP and J’khand; monsoon reaches 22 states
Bhopal/Jaipur/Lucknow/Patna2 hours ago
Heavy rainfall triggered a cloudburst in Uttarakhand’s Chamoli district on Friday, sending debris surging into several homes and shops and trapping multiple vehicles in the affected area.
In separate weather-related incidents, four people were killed by lightning in Balaghat, Madhya Pradesh. In Dewas, a storm accompanied by heavy rain caused a balcony to collapse, killing two women. Ten people died in Jharkhand and two in Chhattisgarh in lightning strikes.
The monsoon has now advanced across 22 states, with forecasts suggesting it could cover the remaining regions by 5 July. However, its progress has been uneven, arriving eight days late in Uttar Pradesh. While it typically reaches the state by 20 June, it has remained stalled near the Bihar border for around 15 days and is expected to move into Uttar Pradesh within the next couple of days.
Pictures of rain from across the country
Heavy rain occurred in Shimla, Himachal Pradesh on Friday afternoon and darkness descended during the day.
A Thar car got stuck in debris after a cloudburst in Chamoli, Uttarakhand.
A portion of a house balcony collapsed in Dewas, Madhya Pradesh. This resulted in the death of 2 women.
Heatwave impact in 6 states, temperature crosses 40°C
On Wednesday, the temperature remained above 40°C in several cities of Madhya Pradesh, Uttar Pradesh, Rajasthan, Haryana, Delhi and Gujarat. The highest temperature in the country was recorded at 43.4°C in Rohtak, Haryana.
Meanwhile, Lakhimpur Kheri in UP recorded 43.2°C, Banda and Agra 43°C, Sriganganagar in Rajasthan 42.1°C, Gwalior in MP 41.8°C and Ahmedabad in Gujarat recorded 41.7°C.
Weather forecast for the next 2 days
28 June:
- Heavy rainfall will occur in some parts of Assam, Meghalaya, West Bengal and Sikkim.
- Orange alert for rain in coastal areas of Sikkim, Goa, Telangana and Karnataka.
- Warning of heavy rainfall in Andhra Pradesh, Jharkhand, and Odisha as well.
- Heatwave alert for the fourth consecutive day in Madhya Pradesh, Uttar Pradesh and Maharashtra (Nagpur-Amravati region).
29 June:
- Heavy rainfall is expected in Chhattisgarh, Telangana, Odisha, Kerala, Goa, Tamil Nadu, Arunachal Pradesh, Assam, Meghalaya, West Bengal, Sikkim.
- Strong winds may blow at a speed of 50-60kmph in Bihar. Strong winds will blow in several states including Madhya Pradesh, Odisha, East Rajasthan and Tamil Nadu.
- Rain is expected in Rajasthan with strong winds at a speed of 30-40kmph. Heatwave will continue in some areas of Eastern Uttar Pradesh.
State-wise weather conditions
Madhya Pradesh: Rain alert in 43 districts today, monsoon advancement wait extended by 2-3 more days
After entering Madhya Pradesh, the monsoon has stalled. It has not progressed for the last 3 days. The wait for its advancement has extended by another 2-3 days. On Friday, Seoni received 50mm of rainfall. Heavy rain occurred in Shujalpur, Akodia and surrounding areas of Shajapur. Ujjain received 38mm of rainfall.
Uttar Pradesh: Rain alert in 30 districts, monsoon to arrive in next 72 hours; temperature in Lakhimpur-Kheri 43.2°C
Pre-monsoon is active in UP. Today there is a rain alert in 30 districts. According to the Meteorological Department, monsoon rains will begin in UP from June 29. A drop of 8°C to 9°C in temperature can be recorded, which will provide relief to people from the scorching heat. On Friday, Lakhimpur Kheri's temperature was the highest at 43.2°C.
Rajasthan: Warning of storm and rain in 27 districts, pre-monsoon rain continues in 16 districts including Udaipur, Ajmer, Kota
Pre-monsoon rain continues in Rajasthan. On Friday, it rained in 16 districts including Sri Ganganagar, Udaipur, Ajmer, and Kota. The highest rainfall of 60mm was recorded in the Deh area of Nagaur. Chittorgarh, Pratapgarh, and Bhilwara received more than 25mm of rain. Today also, there is a yellow alert for thunderstorms and rain in 27 districts. The maximum temperature in Sri Ganganagar was 42.1°C.
Bihar: Orange alert for storm and rain in 12 districts, all districts will receive rain within 2-4 days; temperature will drop by 4°C
The monsoon has now started becoming active in Bihar. This will now start the rains and provide relief to people from the heat. According to the Meteorological Department, there is an orange alert for thunderstorms and rain in 12 districts today. Winds can blow at a speed of 60kmph. The monsoon will become active across the entire state in the next two-three days. The maximum temperature will drop by up to 4°C.
Punjab: Possibility of monsoon in the first week of July, alert for strong storm across the state today
According to the Meteorological Department, monsoon may enter Punjab-Chandigarh in the first week of July. However, today there is a yellow alert for storms and rain in most cities due to the effect of pre-monsoon rain. During this time, storms of 50kmph may also blow. Friday saw intense heat throughout the state.
Haryana: Average increase of 1.3°C in maximum temperature, Rohtak's mercury at 43.4°C; rain alert in 3 districts today
Intense heat continues in Haryana amid monsoon delay. On Friday, Rohtak's temperature was 43.4°C. The state's maximum temperature increased by an average of 1.3°C. The temperature was 3.3°C above normal. According to the Meteorological Department, today only 3 districts - Panchkula, Ambala and Yamunanagar - may receive light rain.

title: Gujarat ATS seizes 2.08 lakh tramadol tablets worth Rs 8.32 crore; Rajasthan drug trader arrested - India's News.Net
full_text:
Ahmedabad (Gujarat) [India], June 26 (ANI): The Gujarat Anti-Terrorism Squad (ATS) on Friday seized 2.08 lakh Tramadol Hydrochloride tablets, valued at approximately Rs 8.32 crore in the international market, and arrested an alleged drug trafficker from Rajasthan in a major crackdown on the illegal psychotropic drug trade.
According to an official statement, acting on specific intelligence, the ATS learnt that Paresh Parsmal Jain, a resident of Pardi in Valsad originally from Sadri in Rajasthan, had allegedly been illegally trafficking Tramadol for a prolonged period.
The ATS developed the intelligence using technical surveillance and human sources, which revealed that on June 25, the accused had dispatched the consignment from Ankleshwar to Pali, Rajasthan, through an MR Travels luxury bus. According to investigators, the shipment was concealed under a tax invoice issued in the name of New Mahavir Medical, with the Tramadol allegedly disguised as other medicines.
Under the supervision of ATS SP K Siddharth and led by Dy SP SL Chaudhary, multiple ATS teams, assisted by the Ahmedabad Crime Branch and SOG, intercepted the bus near Namaste Circle, Shahibaug.
During the search, officers recovered six cartons containing 416 smaller boxes, all labelled 'Tramadol Hydrochloride LP Tablets 100 mg (SCOVIDOL-100).' The consignment comprised 4,160 strips containing a total of 2,08,000 tablets, which were seized.
Police said the accused had fled to Rajasthan after dispatching the consignment. An ATS team tracked him down and arrested him from Gogunda in Udaipur district.
During interrogation, Jain allegedly disclosed that since May 2026, he had procured 62.54 lakh Tramadol tablets in 25 consignments and sold them across Rajasthan and Uttar Pradesh. Investigators said he obtained a medical stockist licence in May 2026 and allegedly used it primarily to trade in Tramadol.
The ATS further revealed that Jain had previously been booked under the Narcotic Drugs and Psychotropic Substances (NDPS) Act in Haridwar (Uttarakhand) and Thane (Maharashtra) in connection with Codeine cough syrup cases.
Investigators have also found his alleged links to a Vapi Mephedrone seizure made by the Gujarat ATS last month, in which four accused were arrested with 2.38 kg of Mephedrone.
Further investigation is underway to identify the wider supply network and trace additional beneficiaries of the illegal drug trade. (ANI)

title: Gujarat: DRI seizes 2 kg cocaine consignment in Surat; 1 held - India's News.Net
full_text:
New Delhi [India], June 26 (ANI): In continuation of its sustained efforts to curb drug trafficking and dismantle narcotics supply networks, the Directorate of Revenue Intelligence (DRI) on Friday seized 2 kg of cocaine at Surat and arrested one person involved in its illicit trafficking under the relevant provisions of the NDPS Act, 1985.
According to an official release, besides this seizure, DRI has carried out a series of operations at various airports, railway stations, courier terminals, and highways in Mumbai, Delhi, Jaipur, Patna, Cochin, and Ahmedabad, and has cumulatively seized approximately 26 kg of cocaine since last month. During these operations, 22 persons, including 14 foreign nationals, were arrested.
The contraband was trafficked through various modus operandi such as ingestion (swallowing drug-filled capsules or pellets for internal concealment within the body), concealment in household and edible items, soaking them in fabrics, concealment in baggage, etc.
Through coordinated operations, DRI has consistently targeted organised syndicates engaged in the trafficking of narcotic drugs and psychotropic substances, contributing to the Government of India's vision of a Nasha Mukt Bharat (Drug-free India).
Every year, June 26 is observed as the International Day against drug abuse and illicit trafficking. Established by the United Nations in 1987, this day is dedicated internationally to strengthening global action, raising awareness about the devastating impacts of substance abuse, and combating the illegal drug trade.
This comes as Union Home Minister Amit Shah on Friday asked states to adopt a 'detect, deduct and destroy' strategy against drug trafficking networks, calling for a ruthless crackdown on cartels and kingpins while advocating a compassionate approach towards victims of addiction.
Addressing the 10th Apex-Level Meeting of the Narco-Coordination Centre (NCORD), Shah stressed that the next three years would be decisive in India's fight against narcotics and urged states to strengthen coordination, real-time intelligence sharing and enforcement against drug syndicates.
'Today, our country stands at such a delicate juncture in the battle against narcotics that the coming three years will determine whether addiction conquers us or we conquer addiction. For the future of our nation over the next 100 years, we must collectively fight this with unwavering resolve. For this, all governments must come together on a single platform. We must also include saints who guide the public, youth who shape the future, and the power of motherhood,' Shah said. (ANI)
